goal: Inspect /app/weights.pt and /app/dataset.pt, reconstruct the model as RecoveredModel, load the original weights, and compute the baseline MSE.
summary: Phase 1 recovered the architecture and computed the baseline MSE. /app/recover.py defines PositionalEncoding (pe buffer (1, 5000, 128)) and RecoveredModel using nn.TransformerEncoder (3 layers, d_model=128, nhead=4, dim_feedforward=256) and nn.TransformerDecoder (1 layer) wrapped in nn.Linear(64,128)/nn.Linear(128,64). The state dict loads with strict=True (no missing/unexpected keys), torch.jit.script succeeds, eager and scripted outputs match, and baseline_mse = 1.5517076253890991. /app/weights.pt sha256 is unchanged at 71471e71c5a311a897362dd5b77413e7ca2ca59abd00a110bdc2cd3f8584d080.
stepSummaries:
1. Inspect /app/weights.pt and /app/dataset.pt to learn the state-dict keys/shapes and the dataset layout (X, Y tensors). Print a concise summary. [verified]
  2. Implement RecoveredModel that mirrors the state-dict structure, load /app/weights.pt into it, freeze all parameters except output_layer, and compute the original MSE on /app/dataset.pt. Persist the baseline MSE for later comparison. [verified]
reviewerRationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Step 1 was proxy (printed summary), step 2 was a real smoke test (torch.jit.script + load_state_dict + print baseline_mse). The load-bearing artifact (RecoveredModel + load_state_dict pipeline) was exercised end-to-end.

Phase goal was to inspect files, reconstruct the model, and compute baseline MSE. All three deliverables exist: inspect_summary, recover.py with RecoveredModel that loads weights strictly, baseline_mse=1.55.
gateVerdicts.evidence: /app/recover.py exists; baseline_mse populated; weights sha256 unchanged.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Nothing deferred. All planned work for Phase 1 is complete: architecture recovered, model loads strictly, baseline MSE computed and exported.
gateVerdicts.evidence: Both steps in phase 1 are completed.
